message.content:
# Proposed KB Page: highlight.js (npm)

---

## KB Page Outline

### 1. Overview

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Package | `highlight.js` (npm) |
| Repository | https://github.com/highlightjs/highlight.js |
| Latest version (as of 2026-04-23) | 11.11.1 |
| Weekly downloads | ~20.6M |
| Security contact | security@highlightjs.com |

Highlight.js is a syntax highlighting library for the browser and Node.js. It operates on code blocks and does **not** execute the code it highlights. Security exposure is narrowly scoped: it matters primarily when **user-supplied content** (language names or code blocks) is passed to the highlighter without sanitization.

---

### 2. Known Vulnerabilities (Public Advisories)

#### 2a. CVE-2020-26237 — Prototype Pollution via crafted language name

| Field | Detail |
|---|---|
| Advisory | GHSA-vfrc-7r7c-w9mx / CVE-2020-26237 |
| CVSS 3.1 | AV:N/AC:H/PR:L/UI:R/S:C/C:N/I:H/A:N (Medium-High, but high attack complexity) |
| Affected versions | < 9.18.2 and 10.x < 10.1.2 |
| Fixed in | 9.18.2, 10.1.2 |
| Root cause | `languages` and `aliases` internal objects inherited from `Object.prototype`. A crafted HTML code block with a specially chosen language attribute could pollute `Object.prototype`. |
| Scope / Pre-conditions | Only exploitable when (a) user-provided HTML/Markdown is rendered **and** (b) language names are not filtered before being passed to highlight.js. Applications that do not render user-supplied data are **unaffected**. |
| Remediation | Upgrade to ≥ 9.18.2 / ≥ 10.1.2. Alternatively, filter user-supplied language names to an allowlist, or patch internal objects to use `Object.create(null)`. |
| Key references | [PR #2636](https://github.com/highlightjs/highlight.js/pull/2636), [Fix commit 7241013](https://github.com/highlightjs/highlight.js/commit/7241013ae011a585983e176ddc0489a7a52f6bb0) |

#### 2b. ReDoS via multiple grammar regular expressions

| Field | Detail |
|---|---|
| Advisory | GHSA-7wwv-vh3v-89cq (no CVE alias listed) |
| Severity | Not formally scored; practical impact is Denial of Service (freeze/crash) |
| Affected versions | All versions < 10.4.1 (including 9.18.5) |
| Fixed in | 10.4.1 |
| Root cause | Numerous language grammars shipped with the library contained regular expressions susceptible to catastrophic (exponential or polynomial) backtracking on adversarial input. |
| Exponential-backtracking grammars | c/cpp/arduino (c-like), handlebars/htmlbars, gams, perl, jboss-cli, r, erlang-repl, powershell, routeros, livescript*, javascript/typescript* |
| Polynomial-backtracking grammars | kotlin, gcode, d, aspectj, moonscript, coffeescript/livescript, csharp, scilab, crystal, elixir, basic, ebnf, ruby, fortran/irpf90, livecodeserver, yaml, x86asm, dsconfig, markdown, ruleslanguage, xquery, sqf |
| Scope / Pre-conditions | Exploitable only when highlight.js processes **user-provided code content** or when `highlightAuto` is used with affected grammars registered. Client-side: UI freeze/crash. Server-side: event-loop block → DoS. Applications highlighting only trusted/static content are **unaffected**. |
| Remediation | Upgrade to ≥ 10.4.1. If upgrading is impossible, remove or replace affected grammars, or avoid processing untrusted input. |
| Key references | [Fix commit 373b9d8](https://github.com/highlightjs/highlight.js/commit/373b9d862401162e832ce77305e49b859e110f9c) |

---

### 3. Current Security Posture (v11.11.1)

- Both known advisories were fixed in the 10.x line; **v11.11.1 is not affected** by either advisory.
- No open or unfixed GitHub Security Advisories found against the current major version as of this review.
- The project maintains a dedicated security contact (security@highlightjs.com) and has published advisories through GitHub's GHSA process.
